name: B-59 Torpedo Room
description: The forward compartment of the submarine, dominated by four torpedo tubes and the heavy cylindrical forms of the weapons themselves. The T-5 nuclear torpedo — distinguished from the conventional rounds by the yellow band painted around its midsection — has been moved from its storage rack to tube one. The room smells of grease, metal, and the particular chemical sharpness of a weapons system that has been brought to readiness.
atmosphericDetails: The second key receptacle is mounted on the port bulkhead between tubes one and two — its brass housing polished from years of inspection.
A yellow band of paint on tube one's outer door indicates the T-5 is loaded and the outer door is closed but not yet flooded.

Three ratings are present, sitting on the deck with their backs against the tube housing — they have been told to wait, and they are waiting with the stillness of men who have been told something they cannot unknow.
